name: Stochastic Gradient Descent
variation: noisy gradient step on a minibatch
boundary: learning rate, batch size, loss landscape

name: Free Energy Principle
boundary: Markov blanket separating system from environment

name: Karma (Theravāda)
selection: moral law of cause and effect

name: Mycelial Resource Routing
fitness: throughput of nutrients to host trees

name: Keynesian Multiplier
selection: propagates through marginal propensity to consume

name: Quantum Superposition
selection: measurement collapses to eigenstate with Born probability

name: Signal Detection Theory
fitness: hit rate vs false alarm rate

name: Dual Process Theory
selection: effort and stakes route to slower system

name: Social Darwinism (historical)
boundary: ideological frame; widely discredited as descriptive science

name: Kolmogorov Complexity
boundary: choice of universal machine (up to constant)

name: Rhizome (Deleuze & Guattari)
selection: any point connects to any other

name: Embodied Cognition
fitness: task success with reduced internal load

name: Standing Wave Resonance
selection: boundary conditions admit only integer harmonics

name: Fourier Decomposition of Sound
variation: amplitude and phase of each harmonic

name: Stimulated Emission (Laser Physics)
boundary: gain medium, optical cavity, pump threshold

name: Stratigraphic Superposition
variation: successive episodes of deposition and erosion
selection: deeper layers are older unless disturbed

name: Cladistic Classification
selection: parsimony or likelihood selects best tree
boundary: available character matrix, fossil record gaps

name: Taphonomy
selection: only resistant materials in favorable conditions preserve

name: Legal Positivism
selection: validity determined by social fact of enactment, not morality
fitness: systemic coherence within the legal order

name: Common Law Precedent (Stare Decisis)
variation: novel fact patterns presented to courts
selection: analogical reasoning extends or distinguishes prior rulings

name: Law and Economics
selection: rules that minimize total social cost adopted or recommended

name: Clausewitzian Center of Gravity
selection: strike the hub on which all else depends

name: Musashi's Theory of Timing
variation: rhythm, tempo, and interval between actions
selection: seize the interval the opponent cannot adapt to

name: Maillard Reaction
variation: temperature, pH, water activity, reactant identity
selection: cascade of intermediates branches into flavor/color compounds

name: Lactic Acid Fermentation
fitness: pH drop preserving food and developing flavor
boundary: anaerobic environment, salt gradient, temperature range

name: Enzyme Kinetics in Cooking
variation: temperature and pH modulate enzyme activity
fitness: desired textural or flavor transformation rate

name: Load Path Analysis
selection: loads follow stiffest continuous path to ground

name: Pattern Language (Alexander)
selection: solutions that resolve conflicting forces adopted

name: Crystal Nucleation
selection: only nuclei exceeding critical radius survive

name: Bragg Diffraction
variation: angle of incidence on periodic planes
selection: constructive interference at integer-wavelength path difference

name: Dislocation Theory
selection: dislocations glide along slip planes under resolved shear stress
boundary: crystal structure, Peierls barrier, grain boundaries

name: Dose-Response Curve
selection: sigmoidal occupancy governed by binding affinity
boundary: receptor density, clearance rate, therapeutic window

name: ADME Pharmacokinetics
fitness: area under the curve within therapeutic window
boundary: organ function, protein binding, genetic polymorphism

name: Lock-and-Key / Induced Fit
selection: complementarity of binding pocket and ligand

name: Vehicle Routing Problem
selection: minimize total distance or cost subject to constraints
boundary: vehicle capacity, time windows, depot location

name: Queueing Theory (M/M/1)
selection: utilization vs wait-time tradeoff at steady state
boundary: arrival rate < service rate for stability

name: Bullwhip Effect
variation: demand forecast errors at each echelon

name: Thermohaline Circulation
selection: density differences drive deep-water formation and overturning
boundary: ocean basin geometry, freshwater input, wind forcing

name: Navier-Stokes Equations
selection: conservation of momentum and mass at every point

name: Turbulent Energy Cascade
variation: kinetic energy injected at large scale
selection: energy transfers to smaller eddies until viscous dissipation
boundary: Reynolds number, injection scale, dissipation scale

name: Galois Theory
selection: group structure determines solvability by radicals

name: Quadratic Reciprocity
selection: reciprocity law constrains mutual quadratic residuosity

name: RSA Cryptosystem
fitness: computational hardness of decryption without key
boundary: key length, polynomial vs exponential time

name: Shannon's Perfect Secrecy
selection: ciphertext is statistically independent of plaintext
boundary: key length >= message length, one-time use

name: Saussurean Sign Theory
variation: arbitrary pairing of sound-image to concept
selection: convention within speech community stabilizes sign

name: Peircean Semiotic Triad
selection: interpretant mediates sign-object relation through inference

name: RNA World Hypothesis
variation: sequence space explored by error-prone copying

name: Drake Equation
selection: product of factors yields expected count
boundary: Milky Way, current epoch, radio-detectable window

name: Panspermia Hypothesis
selection: organisms that tolerate interplanetary transit seed new worlds

name: Dissipative Structures (Prigogine)
selection: entropy production drives self-organization beyond bifurcation
fitness: sustained ordered structure under energy throughput

name: Onsager Reciprocal Relations
variation: conjugate forces (gradients of temperature, chemical potential)
selection: linear response regime couples fluxes symmetrically

name: Maximum Entropy Production Principle
selection: system selects configuration maximizing entropy production rate
boundary: sufficient degrees of freedom, steady-state assumption

name: Integrated Information Theory (IIT)
selection: partition that makes the least difference defines integrated information

name: Higher-Order Thought Theory
selection: meta-cognitive monitoring promotes state to consciousness

name: Circadian Clock (TTFL Model)
fitness: period accuracy and phase-entrainability to light

name: Chronotype Variation
selection: social and ecological schedules favor certain phase preferences
fitness: alignment of internal clock with environmental demands

name: Jenny's Factors of Soil Formation
variation: climate, organisms, relief, parent material, time (CLORPT)
selection: weathering and biological processes differentiate horizons

name: Soil Food Web
selection: substrate quality and predation regulate community structure
boundary: soil moisture, organic matter input, pH

name: Prospect Theory
variation: framing as gain or loss relative to reference point
selection: loss aversion and probability weighting distort expected utility

name: Nudge Theory (Choice Architecture)
selection: architect steers choices without removing options
fitness: behavioral outcome aligned with policy goal

name: Central Place Theory
variation: hexagonal service areas at multiple scales
selection: consumers minimize travel to nearest provider

name: Urban Scaling Laws
selection: superlinear scaling of innovation, sublinear of infrastructure

name: Induced Demand (Braess Paradox)
selection: additional capacity attracts latent demand, restoring congestion

name: SEIR Epidemic Model
variation: latent period, contact rate, recovery rate
selection: exposed compartment delays but does not prevent transmission chain
fitness: epidemic curve shape and peak timing

name: Superspreading Events
selection: a few individuals generate most secondary cases

name: Herd Immunity Threshold
boundary: homogeneous mixing, lifelong immunity, single strain

name: Phlogiston Theory (Historical)
variation: candidate explanations of combustion and calcination
boundary: pre-oxygen paradigm; superseded by Lavoisier's oxidation

name: Alchemical Transmutation
fitness: gold yield (never achieved; led to chemical knowledge)

name: Aristotelian Rhetoric (Ethos/Pathos/Logos)
variation: speaker credibility, audience emotion, logical argument
selection: audience judges credibility, is moved, or convinced

name: Elaboration Likelihood Model
selection: motivation and ability determine central vs peripheral route

name: Trolley Problem / Doctrine of Double Effect
variation: act vs omission, intended vs foreseen harm
selection: deontological vs consequentialist framing yields different verdicts

name: Rawlsian Veil of Ignorance
selection: rational agents behind veil choose maximin

name: Virtue Ethics (Aristotelian)
selection: practical wisdom (phronesis) guides the mean between extremes

name: Kantian Aesthetic Judgment
fitness: reflective harmony of imagination and understanding
boundary: pure aesthetic judgment, free from concept or interest

name: Institutional Theory of Art
selection: conferral of status by artworld representatives
fitness: accepted as art within institutional frame

name: GUM Measurement Uncertainty
variation: Type A (statistical) and Type B (systematic) sources
selection: combined standard uncertainty propagated through model
boundary: measurement model validity, known influence quantities

name: Metrological Traceability
variation: chain of comparisons from instrument to SI realization
selection: each link adds uncertainty; chain must be unbroken
fitness: stated uncertainty traceable to primary standard
boundary: SI definitions, calibration interval, environmental conditions

name: Yoneda Lemma
selection: natural transformations biject with elements of F(A)
fitness: full embedding of category into presheaf category

name: Adjoint Functors
fitness: universal solution to a mapping problem

name: Monads in Category Theory
variation: candidate algebraic structures on a category

name: Curry-Howard Correspondence
variation: programs in a typed lambda calculus
boundary: constructive logic, simply-typed or dependent types

name: Linear Type Systems
boundary: linear logic foundation, no implicit copying or discard

name: Minimum Entropy Production (Near-Equilibrium)
selection: system relaxes to state of minimum entropy production rate
boundary: linear regime, fixed boundary conditions, Onsager symmetry

name: Oral-Formulaic Composition
variation: stock phrases adapted to metrical slot
selection: performer selects formula fitting meter and narrative need
boundary: oral tradition, fixed meter, illiterate composition

name: Seismic Wave Propagation
variation: P-wave and S-wave velocity in layered media
selection: Snell's law and impedance contrasts determine reflection/refraction
boundary: Earth's layered rheology, source depth and mechanism

name: Wavelength-Division Multiplexing
variation: carrier wavelengths packed into bandwidth window
selection: channel spacing limited by crosstalk and amplifier bandwidth
boundary: fiber dispersion, amplifier gain flatness, nonlinearity

name: Harris Matrix (Archaeological Stratigraphy)
selection: physical superposition and cutting relations order contexts

name: Natural Law Theory
variation: candidate principles derived from human nature or reason
selection: unjust laws fail to bind as genuine law
fitness: alignment of positive law with higher moral order

name: Decision Noise (Kahneman)
variation: unwanted variability across judges and occasions
selection: noise adds error orthogonal to bias

name: CBT Cognitive Model
selection: challenging distortions alters emotional and behavioral outcomes
boundary: client motivation, severity of disorder, therapist skill

name: Psychoanalytic Defense Mechanisms
variation: types of defense (repression, projection, rationalization, sublimation)
selection: unconscious ego deploys defenses to reduce anxiety

name: Exposure Therapy (Extinction Learning)
variation: feared stimulus presented without aversive outcome
boundary: context specificity, return of fear, timing of sessions

name: Cognitive Dissonance (Festinger)
selection: discomfort motivates belief change, rationalization, or behavior change
boundary: individual commitment, public vs private behavior

name: Social Identity Theory (Tajfel)
selection: positive distinctiveness motive drives in-group favoritism
boundary: group boundaries, intergroup context, cultural norms

name: Bystander Effect (Diffusion of Responsibility)
variation: group size and ambiguity of situation
selection: responsibility diffuses across group; social proof signals inaction

name: Conformity (Asch)
selection: normative and informational social influence override private judgment
boundary: unambiguous task, unanimous majority, public response

name: Milgram Obedience to Authority
variation: proximity of authority, victim, and degree of legitimacy
selection: agentic state transfers moral responsibility to authority

name: Erikson Psychosocial Stages
selection: successful resolution produces virtue; failure produces maladaptation
fitness: ego integrity and adaptive capacity at each stage
boundary: cultural context, lifespan, quality of caregiving

name: Theory of Mind
selection: developmental emergence around age 4 enables predicting others
boundary: developmental window, neurotypical development, cultural support

name: Self-Determination Theory (Deci & Ryan)
variation: degree of autonomy, competence, and relatedness satisfaction
selection: intrinsic vs extrinsic regulation on autonomy continuum
boundary: social context, need support, controlling vs informational feedback

name: Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs
selection: lower-level needs must be satisfied before higher ones motivate
boundary: cultural universality debated, prepotency sequence not rigid

name: Working Memory Model (Baddeley)
unit: memory subsystem (phonological loop, visuospatial sketchpad, episodic buffer, central executive)
variation: load and modality of incoming information
selection: central executive allocates limited capacity across slave systems
fitness: task performance under concurrent cognitive demands
boundary: capacity limits (~7+/-2 chunks), decay time, modality

name: Spacing Effect (Distributed Practice)
selection: spaced retrieval desirable difficulty outperforms massed practice
boundary: interval must not exceed forgetting threshold, material complexity

name: Encoding Specificity Principle
variation: match between encoding context and retrieval cue
selection: retrieval succeeds when cues reinstate encoding context

name: Big Five / Five Factor Model
variation: trait levels across Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Agreeableness, Neuroticism
selection: factor analysis of trait adjectives converges on five orthogonal factors
fitness: predictive validity for behavior, health, and life outcomes

name: Learned Helplessness / Learned Optimism (Seligman)
selection: uncontrollable aversive events install helplessness; cognitive retraining reverses it
boundary: repeated uncontrollable exposure, cognitive accessibility of attributions

name: Gestalt Principles of Grouping
variation: proximity, similarity, closure, continuity, common fate
selection: law of Pragnanz: percept minimizes complexity
boundary: static and dynamic visual fields, limited to perceptual level

name: General Adaptation Syndrome (Selye)
selection: alarm to resistance to exhaustion sequence unfolds under sustained stress
boundary: stressor duration; chronic stress exhausts adaptive reserves

name: Allostatic Load
variation: repeated stress activation and imperfect recovery
selection: allostasis achieves stability through change; repeated mobilization accumulates damage
boundary: individual stress history, social determinants of health

name: Gift Economy (Mauss)
variation: gift types, timing, and reciprocity obligation
selection: obligation to give, receive, and reciprocate creates social bonds

name: Liminality (Turner)
selection: threshold state dissolves old structure and enables transformation

name: Plant Hormonal Signaling (Auxin)
selection: high auxin promotes cell elongation; polar transport creates directionality
boundary: concentration range, receptor saturation, competing hormones

name: Phyllotaxis
selection: new primordium forms at position of least inhibition (golden angle ~137.5 degrees)

name: Tobler's First Law of Geography
selection: near things are more related than distant things

name: PID Control
selection: weighted sum of error, accumulated error, and error rate minimizes deviation
boundary: linear plant, noise, actuator limits, sampling rate

name: Feedback Linearization
selection: exact cancellation transforms system to linear normal form
fitness: linear controllability of originally nonlinear plant
boundary: full state measurement, relative degree well-defined, no zero dynamics instability

name: Expected Utility Theory
selection: rational agent maximizes probability-weighted utility sum
boundary: von Neumann-Morgenstern axioms, known probabilities, stable preferences

name: Minimax Regret
variation: regret matrix across actions and states of the world
boundary: finite action and state space, no probabilities assumed

name: Demographic Transition Model
variation: death rates and birth rates over development stages
selection: mortality falls first with development; fertility follows with lag
boundary: economic development trajectory, cultural norms, policy

name: HPA Axis Hormonal Feedback Loop
variation: stressor input and glucocorticoid receptor sensitivity
selection: cortisol negative feedback suppresses CRH and ACTH release
boundary: receptor downregulation under chronic stress, circadian rhythm

name: Eusociality and Hamilton's Rule (Kin Selection)
variation: coefficient of relatedness r, benefit b, cost c
selection: altruism spreads when rb > c (inclusive fitness benefit exceeds cost)
boundary: haplodiploidy or repeated interactions in nest, r measurable

name: Locard's Exchange Principle
selection: every contact leaves a trace; transfer is bidirectional

name: Fractal Self-Similarity (Mandelbrot)
selection: statistical or exact self-similarity recurs across scales
fitness: fractal dimension D exceeds topological dimension
boundary: finite inner and outer scales cut off fractal behavior

name: MDA Framework (Mechanics-Dynamics-Aesthetics)
variation: mechanics specified by designer, dynamics emerge from play, aesthetics felt by player
selection: player experience (aesthetics) selects for retained mechanics
boundary: designer controls mechanics; emergent dynamics are indirect

name: Stream Power Erosion Model
variation: discharge, channel slope, and substrate erodibility
selection: erosion rate proportional to unit stream power exceeding threshold
fitness: channel incision and landscape denudation rate
boundary: bedrock vs alluvial channel, discharge variability, tectonic uplift

name: Euler's Theorem (Graph Theory)
selection: Eulerian circuit exists iff all vertices have even degree
fitness: existence of a single traversal covering all edges
boundary: connected graph, undirected or directed variant

name: Graph Coloring (Chromatic Number)
selection: minimize colors so no two adjacent vertices share a color
boundary: NP-hard in general; planar graphs require at most 4 colors

name: Ranganathan's Five Laws of Library Science
variation: access, relevance, and efficiency of knowledge retrieval
selection: laws select for open, efficient, growing system design
fitness: every reader their book; every book its reader; save time
boundary: physical or digital collection, user population

name: Clausewitz's Friction of War
variation: fog of war, chance, and human factors introduce resistance
selection: operational plans degrade; robust strategy accounts for friction
boundary: actual combat conditions, information limits, morale

name: Lanchester's Laws of Combat Attrition
selection: square law: attrition advantage scales with force-size squared for ranged combat
fitness: surviving force size at battle end
boundary: homogeneous units, continuous engagement, no terrain effects

name: Campbell's Monomyth (Hero's Journey)
variation: cultural surface details across world mythologies
boundary: voluntary vs imposed journey; genre scope debated

name: Red Queen Hypothesis
selection: co-evolutionary arms race: rare host genotypes favored, parasite tracks common ones
boundary: sexually reproducing host, obligate parasite, overlapping generations

name: SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping)
selection: probabilistic filter (EKF, particle filter) updates joint pose-map belief
boundary: sensor range and noise, loop-closure detection, dynamic environments

name: Nyquist-Shannon Sampling Theorem
variation: sampling frequency relative to signal bandwidth
selection: aliasing-free reconstruction requires sampling at >= 2 * max frequency
boundary: strictly bandlimited signal, ideal reconstruction filter

name: Two-Process Model of Sleep Regulation (Borbely)
variation: homeostatic sleep pressure (Process S) and circadian drive (Process C)
selection: sleep onset when S exceeds upper threshold; wake when it falls below lower
boundary: circadian period ~24h, ultradian oscillation of REM/NREM within sleep

name: Weaving Tension Dynamics
variation: loom shed angle, pick density, and yarn modulus
selection: tension equilibrium between warp crimp and weft interlacement geometry
fitness: fabric cover factor and dimensional stability
boundary: yarn elasticity, loom geometry, beat-up force

name: Brouwer Fixed-Point Theorem
unit: continuous map on a compact convex set
variation: candidate continuous functions from disk to itself
selection: topological argument (no retraction) guarantees at least one fixed point
fitness: existence of fixed point x such that f(x) = x
boundary: compact convex domain in R^n, continuity required

name: Quasispecies Theory (Eigen)
selection: selection acts on mutant cloud distribution, not single sequence
boundary: error threshold: mutation rate must stay below inverse of genome length

name: Magma Chamber Dynamics
variation: recharge rate, crystallization, volatile exsolution, roof deformation
selection: eruption triggered when chamber pressure exceeds lithostatic threshold
fitness: eruption volume and style (effusive vs explosive)
boundary: crustal rheology, volatile content, recharge flux, reservoir geometry

name: Satisficing (Simon)
selection: first option meeting threshold accepted; optimization abandoned
fitness: decision speed vs optimality tradeoff under bounded rationality
boundary: cognitive limits, search cost, time pressure

name: Prototype Theory (Rosch)
variation: graded membership by similarity to prototype
selection: most typical member anchors the category
boundary: natural categories, fuzzy boundaries, cultural variation

name: Island Biogeography (MacArthur & Wilson)
variation: island area and distance from mainland
selection: immigration and extinction rates reach equilibrium species number
boundary: island size, isolation, colonization ability of species

name: Shannon Channel Capacity
selection: channel capacity C = B log2(1 + S/N) sets hard limit on reliable transmission
boundary: additive white Gaussian noise, fixed bandwidth

name: Comparative Advantage (Ricardo)
selection: specialization in lowest-opportunity-cost goods maximizes mutual gains
boundary: two goods, two countries, constant returns, no transport costs

name: Wave-Particle Duality
selection: measurement context determines whether wave or particle behavior manifests

name: Structural Holes (Burt)
selection: broker bridging structural holes gains information and control advantages

name: Nash Equilibrium
selection: no player can improve payoff by unilateral deviation
boundary: finite players, complete information, mixed strategies allowed

name: Morphogen Gradient (Turing-Wolpert)
selection: cells interpret threshold concentrations as fate instructions
boundary: source-sink geometry, diffusion rate, threshold precision
